raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
In March 2026, total expenditure/revenue came in at £20,928 against a budget of £15,465, producing a net overage of +£5,463 (+35.3%). The variance is concentrated in two lines — Sales and Purchases — both of which are attributable to a single commercial event: the early close of the Meridian Group contract, originally budgeted to land in April 2026. Meridian requested the pull-forward to align with their own financial year-end of 31 March. This accelerated £5,003 of revenue into the period and triggered £750 of associated deployment materials spend against a purchases budget set for a quieter month. A further £1,250 purchase order (PO-2026-0312) covering the full scope of Meridian deployment materials has been formally approved by the Operations Director, accounting for the full purchases actual. All remaining line items are immaterial. The revenue outperformance is expected to reduce the April budget versus actuals comparison as the Meridian deal will no longer close in that month.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,003 (+83.4%) | Budget: £6,000 | Actual: £11,003
The overage is driven entirely by the accelerated close of the Meridian Group contract, pulled forward from 15 April to 24 March at the customer's request to align with their 31 March financial year-end. The CFO and CRO attended the contract signing on 24 March; the MSA was countersigned by the CFO on 25 March. Per the revenue recognition memo referenced in the CRM, £2,000 (first month recurring at £800 plus the £1,200 one-time onboarding fee) and £3,003 of accelerated annual revenue were recognised in March, totalling £5,003 — reconciling the variance exactly. The April Sales line is likely to show an offsetting underperformance against budget as this deal will no longer feature there; confirmation against the April forecast is suggested.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Meridian Group — Stage: Closed Won. Close date moved from 15 April to 24 March. ARR: £4,800. One-time onboarding: £1,200. Pulled forward by 22 days.
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:45:00Z - crm-sales-narrow - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Revenue split — recurring £800/mo + one-time £1,200. Total March recognition: £2,000 first month + £3,003 accelerated annual. See revenue recognition memo.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-03-24-meridian: RE: Meridian Group — Contract execution. Signed MSA received from Meridian legal on 24 March. Revenue recognition memo attached. CFO countersigned 25 March.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-03-24: @channel Q1 close update — Meridian contract signed today, ahead of April target. Deal pulled forward at customer's request to beat their FY end. Total contract value £5,100 (one-time plus first month recurring).
- Calendar - 2024-11-05T22:15:00Z - calendar-sales-broad - cal-2026-03-24: Meridian contract signing — 14:00 BST. CFO and CRO in attendance. Scheduled at customer's request to align with their financial year close 31 March.

Purchases | +£750 (+150.0%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £1,250
The overage is directly linked to deployment materials procured for the Meridian Group onboarding. Purchase order PO-2026-0312, totalling £1,250, was formally approved by the Operations Director on 26 March and matches the actual exactly. The broader COGS reconciliation email noted that this spend was expected given the revenue outperformance in the same period. The Slack message references £750 for initial deployment materials, which may reflect a subset of the total PO scope; the full £1,250 PO is the figure of record. Spend appears sanctioned and process-compliant; no unexplained residual remains.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:45:00Z - gmail-purchases-narrow - gmail-2026-03-26-po: Purchase order PO-2026-0312 approved by Operations Director — £1,250 for deployment materials.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:15:00Z - gmail-purchases-broad - gmail-2026-03-26-cogs: COGS reconciliation for March — direct materials for Meridian onboarding posted in month. Variance is expected given revenue outperformance.
- Slack - 2024-11-09T12:15:00Z - slack-purchases-broad - ops-slack-2026-03-26: Stock order for Meridian onboarding raised — £750 materials for initial deployment. Standard procurement channel, PO attached.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines carried immaterial variances in March 2026 and have not been subject to detailed investigation:
- Advertising & Marketing: +£45 (+0.6%)
- Audit & Accountancy fees: -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees: -£30 (-100.0%)
- Cleaning: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 0%: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% business: +£18 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses: +£124 (+103.6%)
- Light Power Heating: +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses: -£6 (-4.8%)
- Postage Freight Courier: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery: +£46 (+227.9%)
- Rent: -£46 (-4.4%)
- Repairs Maintenance: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Subscriptions: £0 (+0.0%)
- Telephone Internet: -£2 (-4.6%)
- Travel National: -£150 (-100.0%)
Several of these lines show 100% variances (Bank Fees, Cleaning, Entertainment - 0%, Postage Freight Courier, Repairs Maintenance, Travel National) likely reflecting budgeted spend that did not occur in the month, and General Expenses and Printing Stationery show elevated percentage variances on small absolute bases. None breach materiality thresholds in the context of this report, though a periodic review of nil-spend budget lines is suggested to maintain forecast accuracy.
